title: Snake Robot Multi-Servo Joint Chain
design_question: How to build a bio-inspired snake robot with Android-controlled serpentine locomotion?
selected_approach: 8-segment snake: 7 MG996R servo joints (±60°), mounted alternately horizontal/vertical, linked by 3D-printed ABS link bodies (50 mm each). Arduino Mega receives HC-05 Bluetooth commands from Android app (amplitude A, period T, phase offset φ). Servo angles computed as θᵢ = A·sin(2πt/T + i·π/4) + trim.
rationale: MG996R servos provide 9.4 kg·cm at 6 V — sufficient for 60 g link bodies. HC-05 BT eliminates RF licensing complexity. Arduino Mega has 12 dedicated PWM outputs for servos. 8-joint design enables lateral undulation, rectilinear, and sidewinding gaits.

alternatives.reason_rejected: Friction losses in long cable runs.

verification_notes: FACT: 'Snake Robot — Serpentine Locomotion Kinematics' provides joint angle equation and gait parameters.
supporting_fact_titles: Android Bluetooth SPP to Arduino Serial Bridge
